title: Outdoor Structures Additional Information
description: Please provide comprehensive details about your outdoor structure project to ensure compliance with safety, building codes, and community standards

primary.summary: All detached, stand-alone outbuildings must be designed consistent with the main house style and require ARB review and approval. Prefabricated outbuildings are prohibited.
keyRequirements: ARB review and approval required for all outdoor structures
Design must be consistent with main house architectural style
Garages, workshops, pool houses, summer-kitchen structures require specific approval
All structures subject to setback requirements and lot coverage limits
primary.quote: Detached, stand-alone outbuildings are acceptable at Markland and must be designed in a manner consistent with the style of the main house.

additionalReferences.summary: No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive final approval. Refundable performance deposit required.
keyProvisions: Completed Design Review Application Form required
Refundable Performance Deposit required (amount on application form)
Site plan, landscape plan, and architectural plans must be submitted
ARB inspection required before deposit refund

additionalReferences.section: Section 7.9 Fountains & Garden Ornaments / Section 7.10 Gazebos and Garden Structures
additionalReferences.summary: Gazebos and garden structures are permitted but require ARB approval for design and placement
keyProvisions: All garden structures require ARB approval
Location and size restrictions may apply

additionalReferences.summary: Property owner ultimately responsible for ensuring compliance with all applicable codes, ordinances, regulations, permits and laws
keyProvisions: Fee simple property owner has ultimate responsibility for compliance
Must comply with St. Johns County regulations and codes
All federal, state, and local requirements must be met

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.3.3 - Outbuildings must be consistent with main house style
relevantBylaws.requirement: All outdoor structures require ARB approval and architectural consistency
relevantBylaws.note: Prefabricated structures are specifically prohibited in Markland

fields.label: Primary Structure Type
fields.description: Select the primary type of outdoor structure being constructed

relevantBylaws.requirement: All structure types require ARB approval - gazebos, pool houses, workshops, and garden structures specifically mentioned
relevantBylaws.note: Prefabricated structures are prohibited

fields.label: Structure Purpose and Use
fields.placeholder: Family entertainment, storage, gardening, outdoor dining, etc.
fields.description: Describe the intended use and purpose of the structure

fields.label: Structure Dimensions
fields.placeholder:
Length: 16 feet
Width: 12 feet
Height: 10 feet (peak)
Floor area: 192 square feet
fields.description: Provide detailed dimensions including length, width, height, and total square footage

relevantBylaws.requirement: All structures subject to 35-foot maximum height limit measured from lowest point of finish grade
relevantBylaws.note: Dimensions must be included in required site plan submission to ARB

fields.label: Architectural Style
fields.description: What architectural style best describes the structure design?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.3.3 - Consistency with main house style required
relevantBylaws.requirement: Structure must be designed in a manner consistent with the style of the main house
relevantBylaws.quote: must be designed in a manner consistent with the style of the main house

fields.label: Attachment to Existing Structures
fields.description: How does the structure relate to existing buildings?

fields.label: Lot Type Classification
fields.description: Identify your lot type as requirements vary significantly by lot classification

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 3 - Lot Types and Special Conditions
relevantBylaws.requirement: Different lot types have specific requirements that affect structure placement and design
relevantBylaws.note: Lot classification determines setback requirements, height restrictions, and design standards

relevantBylaws.requirement: All exterior finishes must be reviewed and approved by ARB prior to installation
relevantBylaws.note: Materials must be relevant to architectural style and blend with surrounding structures

fields.label: Foundation System
fields.description: What type of foundation system will support the structure?

relevantBylaws.requirement: Foundation must provide positive drainage and cannot divert water onto adjacent lots
relevantBylaws.note: Minimum 18-inch exposed foundation may be required depending on structure type

fields.label: Framing Materials
fields.placeholder:
Pressure-treated lumber: 2x8 joists, 2x10 beams
Posts: 6x6 pressure-treated
Hardware: Galvanized steel brackets and fasteners
fields.description: Describe all structural framing materials including lumber grades, sizes, and hardware

fields.label: Roofing System (if applicable)
fields.placeholder:
Roof structure: 2x6 rafters on 16-inch centers
Roofing material: Architectural shingles to match house
Gutters: Aluminum seamless gutters
fields.description: Describe roofing structure, materials, and drainage system

relevantBylaws.requirement: Acceptable roofing materials include architectural shingles (30-year minimum), concrete/clay tile, slate, or standing seam metal
relevantBylaws.note: Minimum roof slope 6:12, all roof penetrations must be painted to match roof color

fields.label: Decking/Flooring Materials
fields.placeholder:
Material: Composite decking (Trex Transcend)
Color: Spiced Rum
Fasteners: Hidden fastening system
Support: 16-inch joist spacing
fields.description: Specify decking, flooring, or surface materials with brands and installation details

fields.label: Railing and Safety Systems
fields.placeholder:
Railing height: 36 inches
Material: Aluminum with glass panels
Gates: Self-closing hinges for pool safety
Code compliance: Meets 4-inch sphere rule
fields.description: Describe railings, safety features, and code compliance requirements

fields.label: Exterior Finishes and Colors
fields.placeholder:
Siding: Cedar shake to match house
Stain: Sherwin Williams Semi-Transparent #456
Trim: White painted trim to match house trim
Accents: Natural stone veneer base
fields.description: Describe all exterior finishes, colors, and how they coordinate with existing structures

relevantBylaws.requirement: All exterior finishes and color alterations must be reviewed and approved by ARB prior to installation
relevantBylaws.quote: The palette selected must blend and harmonize with surrounding structures

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.4 - Setback requirements and Section 2.3 - Site Plan requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: Must comply with minimum setbacks and provide accurate site plan showing all property lines and dimensions
relevantBylaws.note: Setback requirements vary by lot type and structure location

fields.label: Detailed Structure Location
fields.placeholder: Located in rear yard, 15 feet from house, 25 feet from rear property line, accessible from kitchen sliding door...
fields.description: Provide specific location details including distances from house, property lines, and access points

fields.label: Setback Measurements
fields.placeholder:
Front setback: N/A (rear yard location)
Side setbacks: 12 feet left, 8 feet right
Rear setback: 25 feet
House distance: 15 feet
fields.description: Provide exact measurements from structure to all property lines and existing buildings

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.4 and approved St. Johns County engineering plans
relevantBylaws.requirement: Must comply with all minimum setbacks noted on approved engineering plans and Southaven PUD Zoning Ordinance
relevantBylaws.note: Minimum front setback generally 20 feet for conventional homes, varies by lot type

fields.label: Lot Coverage Impact
fields.placeholder:
Structure adds 192 sq ft to lot coverage
Current coverage: 25%
New total coverage: 27%
Maximum allowed: 35%
fields.description: Calculate how the structure affects total lot coverage percentage

relevantBylaws.requirement: Must comply with maximum lot coverage limits as specified in zoning ordinance
relevantBylaws.note: ARB may require larger setbacks on certain lots

fields.label: Height Restriction Compliance
options: Structure complies with all height restrictions
fields.description: Does the structure comply with local and HOA height restrictions?

relevantBylaws.requirement: Maximum building height for all structures is 35 feet measured from lowest point of finish grade
relevantBylaws.note: No structure shall exceed three (3) stories including attic rooms

fields.label: Access and Circulation
fields.placeholder: Primary access from house through sliding door, secondary access via garden path, emergency egress via side yard...
fields.description: Describe how people will access the structure and emergency egress routes

fields.label: Easement Considerations
fields.description: Does the structure location consider all property easements?

relevantBylaws.requirement: No buildings or walls shall encroach upon an easement. ARB approval required for any improvements within easements
relevantBylaws.note: Replacement of any improvements within easements is responsibility of property owner

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.2.3 - Construction Activities and Section 4.4 - Site Improvements
relevantBylaws.requirement: Must coordinate with existing utilities and call for utility locates to avoid damage
relevantBylaws.note: Owner/Builder responsible for any damage to existing utilities

fields.label: Electrical Requirements
fields.description: What electrical work is needed for this structure?

fields.label: Electrical Installation Details
fields.placeholder: New 20-amp circuit from main panel, GFCI outlets, LED recessed lighting, ceiling fan outlet...
fields.description: Provide details about electrical installation including circuits, outlets, and lighting

fields.label: Plumbing Requirements
fields.description: Does the structure require any plumbing connections?

fields.label: Plumbing Installation Details
fields.placeholder: Hot and cold water lines for outdoor kitchen, floor drain, gas line for grill...
fields.description: Describe any plumbing connections, fixtures, or special requirements

fields.label: Underground Utility Conflicts
options: Known utility conflicts to work around
fields.description: Have underground utilities been located for safe excavation?

relevantBylaws.requirement: Owner/Builder responsible for ensuring no disruption to existing utility systems and must call for utility locates
relevantBylaws.note: Cost for repairs to damage to existing utilities shall be paid by Owner/Builder that caused damage

fields.label: Irrigation System Considerations
options: Will coordinate with common area irrigation
fields.description: How does construction affect existing irrigation systems?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.4.c - Irrigation sleeves required under driveways
relevantBylaws.requirement: Must install irrigation sleeves under any new driveways and coordinate with common area irrigation
relevantBylaws.note: Responsible for any damage to common area irrigation during construction

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.6 - Earthwork and Drainage and Section 5.2.3 - Conservation Lots
relevantBylaws.requirement: Must provide positive drainage and protect conservation areas and existing vegetation
relevantBylaws.note: No disturbance allowed in conservation areas; silt fencing required during construction

fields.label: Drainage and Water Management
fields.placeholder: Structure elevated 6 inches above grade, roof drainage directed to rain garden, permeable surfaces used...
fields.description: How will the structure manage rainwater and avoid drainage problems?

relevantBylaws.requirement: Positive drainage must be provided and drainage cannot be diverted or directed onto adjacent lots
relevantBylaws.note: Downspouts must include subsurface drain pipe or splash blocks to convey water away from foundation

fields.label: Soil and Site Conditions
fields.description: What are the soil and site conditions for construction?

fields.label: Tree and Vegetation Impact
fields.placeholder: One small tree removal required, existing shrubs relocated, 20-foot oak protected with barrier during construction...
fields.description: Describe impact on existing trees and vegetation, including protection measures

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.5 - Existing Vegetation and Tree Protection
relevantBylaws.requirement: Protected trees must be identified, located, and properly protected with silt barriers before site work
relevantBylaws.note: No disturbance permitted within drip line of protected tree species

fields.label: Conservation Area Considerations
fields.description: Does your lot adjoin conservation lands or wetlands?

relevantBylaws.requirement: No disturbance of any kind in conservation lands; continuous silt fence required along conservation interface
relevantBylaws.note: Conservation easement granted to ACOE and SJRWMD - any disturbance requires immediate restoration at owner expense

fields.label: Environmental Considerations
fields.description: Environmental features incorporated into the structure design

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.2.1 - Construction Hours and Section 4.2.3 - Construction Activities
relevantBylaws.requirement: Construction hours must comply with St. Johns County regulations; ARB reserves right to limit weekend/holiday hours
relevantBylaws.note: Clean construction site must be maintained at all times

fields.label: Design Professional
fields.description: Who designed the structure?

relevantBylaws.requirement: Prefabricated outbuildings are not permitted at Markland
relevantBylaws.note: All structures must be custom designed consistent with main house style

fields.label: Design Professional Information
fields.placeholder: John Smith Architecture, License #FL-AA12345
fields.description: Name and license of design professional (if applicable)

fields.label: Construction Method
fields.description: Who will construct the structure?

fields.label: Contractor Information
fields.placeholder: ABC Construction LLC - License #FL-CCC123456
fields.description: Name and license of construction contractor (if using professional)

fields.label: Contractor Insurance Coverage
fields.placeholder: General liability: $1M, Workers comp: $500K, Bonded: $100K
fields.description: Contractor insurance and bonding information

fields.label: Construction Start Date
fields.description: When will construction begin?

fields.label: Construction Duration
fields.description: How long will construction take?

fields.label: Construction Work Schedule
fields.placeholder: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM, No Sunday work, No work during HOA events
fields.description: Specify days and hours when construction work will occur

relevantBylaws.requirement: Construction hours shall generally comply with St. Johns County regulations
relevantBylaws.note: Developer and Association reserve right to limit construction hours on weekends and holidays as residents occupy community

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 1 - Document Purpose and Section 4.1 - Responsibility for Compliance
relevantBylaws.requirement: All construction must conform to Markland Declaration and all applicable governmental codes, ordinances, regulations, permits and laws
relevantBylaws.note: Property owner ultimately responsible for ensuring compliance with all requirements

fields.label: Building Permit Requirements
options: No permit required per local code
Permit required - will apply after HOA approval
fields.description: What is the building permit status for this structure?

fields.label: Permit Details and Numbers
fields.placeholder: Building permit #BP2024-1234, Electrical permit #EP2024-5678
fields.description: Provide permit numbers if permits have been obtained

fields.label: Building Code Compliance
fields.description: Check all building code requirements that apply to this structure

fields.label: Special Inspections Required
fields.description: Check any special inspections required during construction

fields.label: ARB Application Status
fields.description: What is the current status of your ARB application?

relevantBylaws.requirement: No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive their final approval
relevantBylaws.note: All required submittal documents must be complete before ARB review

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 1 - Document Purpose and Section 6.1 - Architectural Character
relevantBylaws.requirement: ARB evaluates each application for total effect and overall aesthetic impact; must maintain architectural character of community
relevantBylaws.note: Approval of one application does not create obligation to approve similar applications

fields.label: HOA Architectural Guidelines Compliance
options: Structure fully complies with all guidelines
Structure mostly complies with minor variations
fields.description: How does the structure align with community architectural guidelines?

relevantBylaws.requirement: ARB may waive specific provisions if overall aesthetic impact is acceptable, but may also deny applications that meet criteria if aesthetic impact is unacceptable
relevantBylaws.note: Matters of judgment and taste cannot be reduced to simple measurable criteria

fields.label: Architectural Harmony with Existing Home
fields.placeholder: Structure materials and colors match house exterior, roofline complements main house, design maintains consistent architectural theme...
fields.description: Explain how the structure complements your home's existing architecture

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.3.c - All exterior elevations consistently designed
relevantBylaws.requirement: All exterior elevations of each residence shall be consistently designed and implemented
relevantBylaws.note: Design elements used on front elevation shall be incorporated on all elevations

fields.label: Neighborhood Visual Compatibility
fields.placeholder: Structure size and style consistent with neighborhood character, maintains open feel, enhances property appearance...
fields.description: How does the structure fit with the overall neighborhood character?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.4.8.e - Palette must blend and harmonize with surrounding structures
relevantBylaws.requirement: The palette selected must blend and harmonize with surrounding structures
relevantBylaws.note: Colors in the range of pink, purple and black will not be approved on exterior of residence

fields.label: Neighbor Impact Assessment
fields.placeholder: Structure does not block neighbor views, construction noise minimized, no privacy impacts, maintains property line setbacks...
fields.description: Assess potential impacts on neighboring properties including views, privacy, and noise

fields.label: Neighbor Communication
options: All adjacent neighbors informed and supportive
Neighbors informed with generally positive response
Neighbors informed with some concerns addressed
fields.description: Have you communicated with neighbors about the structure?

fields.label: Safety Features and Considerations
fields.description: Safety features incorporated into the structure design

fields.label: Long-term Maintenance Plan
fields.placeholder: Annual: cleaning and inspection, Bi-annual: staining/sealing, Every 5 years: structural inspection, As needed: hardware tightening...
fields.description: Comprehensive plan for ongoing structure maintenance

fields.label: Insurance and Warranty Considerations
fields.placeholder: Homeowner's insurance updated to include structure, contractor provides 1-year warranty on workmanship...
fields.description: Insurance coverage and warranty information for the structure

fields.label: Future Expansion Possibilities
fields.placeholder: Structure designed to accommodate future screen enclosure, electrical planned for future outdoor kitchen addition...
fields.description: How does the current design accommodate potential future modifications?

required_documents: Completed Design Review Application Form (provided by ARB)
Refundable Performance Deposit (amount specified on application form)
Site plan with accurate lot dimensions, north arrow, and scale (1"=10' or 1"=20')
Accurate street frontage information including street address and lot number
Property lines, easement lines, setback lines, and structure location
Architectural plans showing all exterior elevations and materials
Colors and materials samples (digital samples acceptable)
Landscape plan if structure impacts landscaping
Contractor license and insurance certificates (if using contractor)

criticalReminders: NO CONSTRUCTION may commence until final ARB approval is received
Refundable Performance Deposit required - amount posted on application form
All structures must be consistent with main house architectural style
Prefabricated outbuildings are PROHIBITED at Markland
Maximum building height is 35 feet measured from lowest point of finish grade
All exterior finishes and colors must be ARB approved prior to installation
Must comply with all setback requirements which vary by lot type
Silt fencing required during construction adjacent to conservation areas
Call 811 for utility locates before any excavation
Construction hours subject to St. Johns County regulations and ARB limitations
commonViolations: Starting construction before receiving final ARB approval
Failing to match architectural style of main house
Incorrect setback measurements or easement encroachments
Not obtaining required building permits before construction
Damaging existing utilities or common area improvements
Inadequate drainage planning resulting in water runoff to adjacent lots
Failing to protect existing trees and vegetation during construction
Not maintaining required silt fencing and erosion controls

specialConsiderations.lotTypeRequirements: Different lot types (Conventional, Traditional Type 1 & 2, Lakefront, etc.) have specific requirements for setbacks, design standards, and approval processes
specialConsiderations.conservationAreas: Lots adjacent to conservation areas require special protection measures including continuous silt fencing and no disturbance in conservation lands
specialConsiderations.performanceDeposit: Deposit is refundable only after successful completion of all work and compliance inspection by ARB
specialConsiderations.arbDiscretion: ARB has sole discretion to approve or deny applications based on overall aesthetic impact, even if individual criteria are met
